 {"id":"bd-1iwi","title":"Workstream: Interactive Editor UX Parity (autocomplete, multiline, bang, images)","description":"# Goal
Bring the **interactive editor experience** to parity with legacy pi-mono, focusing on:
- Autocomplete (commands, templates, skills, file references, path completion)
- Multi-line editing behavior (Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits)
- Bash shortcuts (`!` and `!!`)
- Clipboard image paste + drag/drop attachments

# Legacy Behavior (Source-of-Truth)
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Editor”:
- File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files
- Path completion: Tab to complete paths
- Multi-line: Shift+Enter (Ctrl+Enter on Windows Terminal)
- Images: Ctrl+V to paste, or drag onto terminal
- Bash commands: `!command` runs and sends output to LLM, `!!command` runs without sending

Legacy implementation details:
- Autocomplete is provided by `CombinedAutocompleteProvider` (slash commands, prompt templates, extension commands, skill commands, file path completion using `fd`).
- Image paste writes clipboard image to a temp file and inserts the file path into the editor.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/interactive.rs` reserves Tab for future autocomplete but has no autocomplete UI.
- No `@` file fuzzy search.
- No Shift+Enter newline semantics (current behavior differs).
- No `!` / `!!` bash shortcuts.
- No clipboard image paste handler.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Autocomplete engine
- Provide suggestions for:
  - Slash commands (built-in)
  - Prompt templates (`/<template>`)
  - Skill commands (`/skill:name`) when enabled
  - File references after typing `@` (fuzzy search project files)
  - Path completion when cursor is inside a path token

## 2) Autocomplete UI
- Render a dropdown/list with:
  - configurable max visible items (`autocomplete_max_visible`)
  - keyboard navigation
  - selection/accept behavior

## 3) Multi-line input semantics
- Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits.
- Windows Terminal: Ctrl+Enter inserts newline.

## 4) Bash shortcuts
- `!cmd`: run and include output in LLM context.
- `!!cmd`: run but do not include output in LLM context.

## 5) Clipboard image paste and drag/drop
- Ctrl+V attempts to read clipboard image:
  - if an image exists, write it to a temp file and insert the file path at cursor.
  - do nothing (silent) if clipboard has no image or access denied.
- Drag/drop: terminals usually paste a file path; ensure we handle file paths cleanly.

# Testing
- Unit tests for autocomplete matching and insertion.
- State tests for key interactions (Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+V).
- Integration tests for `!` / `!!` behavior (using deterministic command runner where possible).

# Dependencies
- Keybinding routing (`bd-3ip`) for Tab / Shift+Enter / Ctrl+V behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits
[ ] ! and !! bash shortcuts implemented
[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)

 {"id":"bd-1iy","title":"Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/gemini.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Gemini provider (Google Generative AI):
- generateContent endpoint with streaming
- Function declarations (tools)
- 631 lines, 4 unit tests

## Gemini-Specific Considerations
1. **Streaming URL**: Uses ?alt=sse query parameter
2. **Response Format**: Different JSON structure from Anthropic/OpenAI
3. **Tool Format**: functionDeclarations in tools array
4. **Content Parts**: text, functionCall, functionResponse

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa, bd-pwz, bd-37l (follow Anthropic pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] 4 tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CodexGPT52","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:08.130271194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:47.262503336Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:47.262441120Z","close_reason":"Already migrated to asupersync HTTP + SSE","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.665829753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:08.138703827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-pwz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.413699713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":51,"issue_id":"bd-1iy","author":"CodexGPT52","text":"Heads-up: src/providers/gemini.rs already uses asupersync HTTP client (src/http/client.rs) + src/sse.rs. Once bd-37l is closed (it was blocking this), I plan to close bd-1iy as already complete.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:20:44Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-1iy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified bd-1iy is already complete: src/providers/gemini.rs uses asupersync HTTP client (src/http/client.rs) + canonical SSE parser (src/sse.rs). Closing now that bd-37l is closed.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jdk","title":"Workstream: Model Selector + Scoped Cycling Parity","description":"# Goal
Match legacy model-selection UX:
- `Ctrl+L` opens a model selector.
- `Ctrl+P` / `Shift+Ctrl+P` cycles models forward/back within a scope.
- `/scoped-models` configures the scope for cycling.

# Legacy Spec
- Legacy README: Keyboard Shortcuts and Commands tables.
- Legacy docs:
  - `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md` (`enabledModels` patterns for cycling)

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- Interactive has `/model` command but no selector UI.
- Interactive has `/scoped-models` but:
  - no Ctrl+P cycling behavior
  - scope is not persisted
  - current Ctrl+P/N are used for input history navigation

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Model selector UI
- Show available models (from `ModelRegistry`) with fuzzy search.
- Selecting a model updates agent provider/model + persists to session header.

2) Scoped cycling
- Define the cycling list:
  - if `enabled_models`/`--models` patterns provided → use filtered list
  - else cycle all available models
- Implement forward/backward cycle with stable ordering.

3) `/scoped-models`
- Provide an in-app UI to edit scoped models (enable/disable patterns or pick from list).
- Persist scoped model patterns to settings (`enabled_models`).

# Dependencies
- Keybindings workstream (`bd-3ip`) is required to map Ctrl+L/Ctrl+P consistently.
- Unit tests for model registry scope logic are already tracked in `bd-2yd`.

# Testing
- State tests for model selector open/select.
- Deterministic tests for scoped cycling ordering.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:39.940944943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:00:26.417626551Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:39.947282823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jn","title":"Impl: Runtime hardening (limits, watchdog, accounting)","description":"# Goal
Harden the PiJS runtime so extension execution is **bounded, observable, and safe**.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Memory cap + allocation tracking for QuickJS runtime.
- CPU/time budget via interrupt hook or watchdog.
- Hostcall timeouts enforced centrally.
- Structured metrics: per-extension CPU, memory, hostcall counts.
- Explicit termination reasons with user-friendly messages.

# Security / Reliability Invariants
- Extension cannot exceed configured budgets.
- Violations terminate the extension in a controlled, logged way.

# Tests
- Unit tests for budget exceed -> deterministic error.
- E2E security suite validates enforcement + log output.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:22.240908810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:06:55.297533242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jn","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:49.136502941Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1kza","title":"Interactive: /share uploads session HTML as private GitHub gist","description":"# Goal
Implement gist-backed `/share` parity with legacy.

# Required Behavior
1) Verify `gh` exists.
2) Verify `gh auth status` succeeds.
3) Export session to a temp HTML file.
4) Show a cancellable loader while running:
   - `gh gist create --public=false <tmpFile>`
5) On success:
   - parse the returned gist URL
   - extract gist ID
   - compute viewer URL (see `bd-2uji`)
   - show both viewer URL and gist URL
6) Cleanup temp file.

# Cancellation
- If user cancels while `gh` is running:
  - kill the process
  - restore the editor
  - show status “Share cancelled”

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end with real `gh`.
- [ ] Produces the correct viewer URL.
- [ ] Cancellation never leaves a broken UI state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:13.532989668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:40.863399794Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:13.539543450Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-2v6k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:40.863371100Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357212214Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.395051624Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357181036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352770513Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352674283Z","close_reason":"Session picker uses index with fallback","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.870643664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101795250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vmm","title":"E2E: legacy vs Rust parity runner (fixtures + diff logs)","description":"Background:
- We must prove parity by running identical scenarios in legacy and Rust and diffing outputs.

Steps:
- Build a runner that executes scenarios in both runtimes (legacy + Rust).
- Normalize outputs using the harness rules (paths/time/ANSI) before diffing.
- Emit per-scenario diff logs with context, including raw vs normalized output.
- Store diff artifacts for CI review and triage.

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs (extension id, scenario id, runtime).

Acceptance:
- Parity runner completes for the full sample and produces deterministic diffs.
- Failures are actionable with minimal manual digging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:47:11.638706880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:58.553997960Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:36.025190918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:58.553968786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:50.448234186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:54.640846590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vmm","depends_on_id":"bd-7al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:59:40.204782716Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wdo","title":"Crate publish readiness audit (pi + libs)","description":"Background:
- We plan to publish pi_agent_rust, asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust to crates.io with a tag-driven workflow.
- Rust-crates-publishing skill requires metadata (license/readme/repo), tag/version alignment, and dry-run validation.

Scope / Steps:
1) Audit each Cargo.toml: package metadata, readme path, repository URL, keywords/categories, publish flag.
2) Ensure versioning and tag strategy (vX.Y.Z matches package.version).
3) Add/update GitHub Actions publish job gated on tags + CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN.
4) Run cargo package/publish --dry-run for each crate (order deps first).

Acceptance:
- All crates pass dry-run publish locally.
- CI publish workflow is in place and gated to release tags.
- Version/tag alignment documented in README or RELEASE.md.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.527044029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.543413705Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wdo","depends_on_id":"bd-3tu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:08.543375474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-wkdk","title":"Docs: keybindings.md (actions + config)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/keybindings.md` documenting:
- supported actions
- default bindings
- customization via `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`

# Source Material
- Legacy: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/keybindings.md`

# Must Include
- Key string format (`ctrl+shift+x`, etc.) and supported key names.
- Full action list grouped by category.
- Example configs (Emacs/Vim style).
- Note any Rust-specific differences (e.g., Windows Ctrl+Enter newline).

# Dependencies
- Should reflect implemented keybinding layer (`bd-3ip`).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:48:25.875843791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:51:04.880238984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wkdk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dd3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:51:04.880209590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-wkdk","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:48:25.882495536Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-x85o","title":"Interactive: /tree full navigator UI + branch summarization","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/tree` semantics and interactive UI as described in `legacy .../docs/tree.md`.

# Required UI/Controls (legacy)
- Display a depth-first tree list with indentation.
- Mark the current leaf as `← active`.
- Controls:
  - Up/Down: move selection
  - Enter: select
  - Escape/Ctrl+C: cancel
  - Ctrl+U: toggle user messages only
  - Ctrl+O: toggle show-all (include custom/label entries)

# Selection Behavior (legacy)
- Selecting a **user message** or **custom message**:
  1) leaf set to parent of selected node (or null if root)
  2) message text placed in editor for re-submission
- Selecting a **non-user message**:
  1) leaf set to selected node
  2) editor remains empty

# Branch Summarization (legacy)
Before switching branches, user chooses:
1) No summary
2) Summarize (default prompt)
3) Summarize with custom prompt

Summarize the abandoned path from old leaf back to common ancestor (or stop at compaction node).
Store result as a branch_summary entry and attach to the new leaf.

# Implementation Notes
- Current Rust `/tree` is a non-interactive listing + manual switch; this task replaces that with a selector.
- Reuse existing session tree operations where possible (`Session::list_leaves`, `navigate_to`, `append_branch_summary`).
- Ensure agent context is updated after switching leaf.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/tree` UX and semantics match legacy docs.
- [ ] Optional summary is created/stored correctly.
